seo.title: Amazon Bedrock Agents Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Amazon Bedrock Agents agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams deploying Amazon Bedrock Agents face heightened compliance risks due to strict regulations governing AI in hiring decisions, including EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act classifying such tools as high-risk. Malformed agent responses or biased knowledge base retrievals can inadvertently discriminate against protected classes, triggering lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides essential post-processing guardrails via vex.verify() on invoke_agent() responses to ensure fairness, transparency, and auditability.

complianceRequirements.description: AI screening tools must not discriminate based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability; annual bias audits required.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent outputs for biased language or disparate impact scoring, flagging responses that reference protected attributes during resume screening.

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring decisions and conduct annual bias audits on automated employment decision tools.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs AI usage disclosures in agent sessions and generates audit trails for bias checks on Bedrock Agent knowledge base retrievals.

complianceRequirements.description: High-risk AI systems in employment must ensure transparency, human oversight, and risk mitigation for discriminatory outcomes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces guardrails on Bedrock Agent action groups, validating multi-turn session state preserves candidate consent and oversight logs.

complianceRequirements.description: Resume screening must accommodate disabilities and avoid disparate treatment based on protected characteristics.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans knowledge base chunks and agent responses for ADA-compliant language, blocking discriminatory filters in recruitment workflows.

industryRisks.name: Action group Lambda returning biased candidate rankings
industryRisks.description: Lambda functions in Bedrock Agent action groups compute scores favoring certain demographics due to flawed training data.
industryRisks.example: Agent ranks candidates higher based on 'cultural fit' proxies correlating with race, violating EEOC guidelines.

industryRisks.name: Knowledge base missing diverse candidate documents
industryRisks.description: Chunking strategies in Bedrock Knowledge Bases fail to retrieve resumes from underrepresented groups.
industryRisks.example: Female engineer resumes chunked poorly, not surfaced for senior roles, breaching gender bias laws.

industryRisks.name: Guardrails missing HR policy violations
industryRisks.description: Bedrock Agent guardrails don't catch domain-specific violations like ageist screening prompts.
industryRisks.example: Agent suggests 'digital native' filters excluding candidates over 40, triggering ADEA complaints.

industryRisks.name: Session state loss in multi-turn screening
industryRisks.description: Lost conversation state between invocations forgets candidate AI notification consent.
industryRisks.example: Second-turn agent interaction omits 'AI used in screening' disclosure required by NYC Local Law 144.

implementation.title: Wrap invoke_agent() with Vex Bias Verification
implementation.description: Post-process Bedrock Agent responses to detect discriminatory language in candidate evaluations using Python SDK.
implementation.code:
import boto3
import vex

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-key')

response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
    agentId='your-agent-id',
    agentAliasId='your-alias-id',
    sessionId='candidate-session-123',
    inputText='Screen this resume for software engineer role.'
)

completion = response['completion']

# Verify for EEOC bias violations
result = vex_client.verify(
    'hr-bias-check',
    {
        'input': response['inputText'],
        'output': completion,
        'context': {'domain': 'recruitment', 'protected_classes': ['race', 'gender', 'age']}
    }
)

if not result.passed:
    print('Blocked: Bias detected:', result.issues)
else:
    print('Safe to proceed:', completion)

implementation.title: TypeScript Guardrail for Knowledge Base Retrieval
implementation.description: Validate Bedrock Agent KB retrievals don't miss diverse candidate documents using TypeScript SDK.

implementation.title: Multi-Turn Session State Compliance Check
implementation.description: Ensure Bedrock Agent preserves AI disclosure notifications across conversation turns.
implementation.code:
import boto3
import vex

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key='your-vex-key')

# Multi-turn session
session_id = 'candidate-session-456'

response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
    agentId='your-agent-id',
    agentAliasId='your-alias-id',
    sessionId=session_id,
    inputText='Continue screening - did you disclose AI usage?'
)

# Verify session state compliance
result = vex_client.verify(
    'hr-session-compliance',
    {
        'session_id': session_id,
        'current_turn': response['completion'],
        'required_elements': ['AI disclosure notification', 'candidate consent logged'],
        'domain': 'NYC-LL144'
    }
)

if result.passed:
    print('Session compliant:', response['completion'])
else:
    print('Session reset required:', result.issues)

caseStudyScenario.heading: Amazon Bedrock Agents in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCorp deployed a Bedrock Agent for high-volume software engineer hiring, integrating a knowledge base of 50K+ resumes and Lambda action groups for skills assessment and ranking. The agent screened candidates via conversational interface, retrieving relevant resumes and generating shortlists. However, poor chunking missed diverse candidate profiles, Lambda functions weighted 'cultural fit' proxies correlating with gender, and multi-turn sessions lost AI disclosure tracking required by NYC Local Law 144.

During peak hiring, the agent surfaced predominantly male candidates from top universities, triggering EEOC complaints. Legal review revealed no audit trail for bias checks, exposing TechCorp to $1,500/violation fines and reputational damage.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Implementing Vex guardrails via vex.verify() on invoke_agent() calls blocked 87% of biased responses before candidate notification. Knowledge base diversity scoring ensured minimum representation across protected classes, while session state verification maintained compliance across 10K+ interactions. TechCorp achieved annual bias audit readiness with automated Vex logs, diversified hires by 42%, and eliminated regulatory exposure.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex guardrails into your Bedrock Agents today to eliminate hiring bias risks and ensure regulatory compliance.
